commit.message:
feat: add ResponseSanitizationFilter to strip stack traces from error responses (#1353)

* feat: add ResponseSanitizationFilter to strip stack traces from error responses

Add a servlet filter that sanitizes error responses to prevent Java stack
traces and internal implementation details from leaking to clients.

When an error response (HTTP 4xx/5xx) is detected that contains stack trace
markers, the body is replaced with a generic HTML page showing only the HTTP
status code and a correlation ID. The full original body is logged server-side
with the same correlation ID.

- ResponseSanitizationFilter: captures PrintWriter output via CapturingResponseWrapper,
  detects stack traces via STACK_TRACE_PATTERN, replaces tainted error bodies
- web.xml: registered as outermost filter (first filter-mapping) to intercept
  errors from all downstream layers
- carlos.properties: response.sanitization.enabled=true (default)
- ResponseSanitizationFilterUnitTest: 22 unit tests covering stack trace detection,
  pass-through, sanitization, committed responses, and uncaught exceptions

Compliance: NIST SP 800-53 SI-11, OWASP ASVS 7.4.1, CWE-209

- INT24 native multiply confirmed: 5.73ms < f32 add 6.02ms
- Sub-32-bit NIR ops (u2u8, pack/unpack) all fail — SFN gaps
- GPR occupancy sweet spot: 16-32 GPR per kernel
- KCACHE/LDS crash confirmed as ALU-IS-LDS bug (already fixed)
- OOB robustness: no hardware trap, needs software lowering

feat(benchmarks): add conv_cifar_v3 suite for phase-3 GPU sweep

15 configurations in 7 groups targeting beyond 75% CIFAR-10 accuracy:

1. Random crop augmentation (pad-4 reflect + random crop), the standard
   CIFAR-10 augment used by CNN baselines, as single-model baseline.
2. n_augment sweep (2, 3 copies per batch) — accumulates more
   augmented variants into the normal-equation system.
3. 20k neurons + crop+flip augmentation (single model).
4. Ensemble ×5 + crop+flip with n_augment sweep.
5. Large ensembles (×10, ×20) with hflip and crop+flip.
6. Diverse patch-size ensembles (p3/p5/p7) ×6, ×9 with crop+flip.
7. Combined best: 20k neurons × 5 ensemble + crop+flip.

commit.message:
Fix performance test flake on CI (#152)

Fix flaky `should measure elapsed time accurately` test on CI.

## The Bug

`setTimeout` only guarantees a minimum delay, not a maximum. On busy CI
agents (especially macOS), the callback can fire well beyond the 100ms
tolerance window, causing `assertBelow` to fail.

## The Fix

Remove the upper-bound assertion — only verify that elapsed time is at
least the requested delay. The test still validates that
`performance.now()` returns accurate, monotonically increasing values.

Also removes the now-unused `sanitizersEnabled` plumbing (CMake define,
C++ global, TS declaration) since this was its only consumer.

commit.message:
fix: graceful fallback when GitHub rejects inline review comments (#50)

## Problem

PR #49 review failed with 422 because the CLI generated inline comments
with line numbers not in the PR diff. The entire review was lost.

## Fix

When `post_review()` gets a 422 and has inline comments, it retries
without them — appending the comment text to the review body instead.
The review still gets posted, feedback isn't lost.

## Context

This is a natural consequence of the token refactor (#40) — the CLI
writes review JSON and the orchestrator posts it. The CLI doesn't know
which lines are in the diff, so it may guess wrong. This fallback
ensures the review always gets posted.

commit.message:
fix: register SIGTERM handler before initial sync cycle

Move signal handler registration to the top of main() so that
SIGTERM sent by Docker during the initial startup sync (which can
run for hours across 300+ TLDs) is correctly intercepted.

Previously the handler was only registered after the blocking
scheduler was created, meaning containers receiving SIGTERM during
the startup cycle would ignore it and keep running until SIGKILL
(30-minute grace period). This caused 3 simultaneous worker
containers during rolling deploys.

commit.message:
fix(maglev): fix truncation chain conversion by recursing before checking inputs

The mark_truncated function checked is_provably_i32 on inputs BEFORE
recursively processing them, preventing Generic->Int32 conversion for
chains longer than 1 node. For a chain like:
  GenericBitwiseOr -> GenericSubtract -> GenericAdd -> Phi
the backward walk failed at GenericSubtract because GenericAdd hadn't
been converted yet.

Fix: recursively call mark_truncated on inputs FIRST, then check
is_provably_i32 (inputs may now be in replacements map). This allows
the full arithmetic chain to be converted to wrapping Int32 ops.

Also adds early-return guard for nodes already in replacements to
prevent infinite recursion.

Expected impact: arithmetic_loop benchmark should see major improvement
as the entire (n + i*3 - 1) | 0 chain converts to Int32 ops.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(reporter): cover empty-file snippet branch in Human reporter

Add a test that passes a diagnostic with a line number pointing at an
empty source file. The file exists in MockFs but has zero lines, so
`start_idx < total_lines` (0 < 0) is false and the snippet_data
computation returns None — the branch that was previously uncovered.

Also simplify the guard condition from
  `if start_idx < total_lines && start_idx < end_idx`
to
  `if start_idx < total_lines`

The second sub-condition (`start_idx < end_idx`) is always true whenever
the first is true (provable from the arithmetic), making it dead code and
leaving LLVM with an un-coverable branch 3.  Removing it eliminates the
dead branch and brings reporter.rs to 100% branch coverage.

GPU-CPU particles parity: Support factor2 range randomization in gradients (#18283)

## Description

Upgrades the GPU particle gradient texture pipeline so that all
factor-based gradients (`addSizeGradient`, `addAngularSpeedGradient`,
`addVelocityGradient`, `addLimitVelocityGradient`, `addDragGradient`)
support the optional `factor2` parameter. When `factor2` is set, each
particle interpolates between `factor1` and `factor2` using its stable
per-particle `seed`, producing visible per-particle variation, matching
the CPU `ParticleSystem` behavior.

Previously, the GPU system ignored `factor2` entirely and baked only
`factor1` into a single-channel (R) texture, making all particles
uniform at each gradient stop.

## Changes

### Gradient helper consolidation (`baseParticleSystem.ts`)
- Moved `_addFactorGradient` (with sorting) and `_removeFactorGradient`
from both `GPUParticleSystem` and `ThinParticleSystem` into
`BaseParticleSystem` as shared protected methods, eliminating duplicate
logic

### Engine (`gpuParticleSystem.ts`)
- All five `addXxxGradient` methods now accept the optional `factor2`
parameter
- `_createFactorGradientTexture` produces **RG** (2-channel) float
textures instead of R (1-channel), storing `factor1` in the R channel
and `factor2 ?? factor1` in the G channel
- Simplified `_refreshFactorGradient` by removing redundant sort logic
(now handled by the base helper)
- Removed redundant `_releaseBuffers()` call from the old private
`_addFactorGradient`

### Shaders
- **GLSL** (`gpuUpdateParticles.vertex.fx`): All five gradient types
sample `.rg` and interpolate using per-particle `seed` components
- **WGSL** (`gpuUpdateParticles.compute.fx`): Matching changes for the
WebGPU compute path

### Seed component assignments
| Gradient Type | Seed Component |
|---|---|
| Size | `seed.y` |
| Angular Speed | `seed.z` |
| Velocity | `seed.w` |
| Drag | `seed.x` |
| Limit Velocity | `seed.y` |

### Visual Tests
Added 5 new factor2 range visual tests (CPU vs GPU side-by-side):

| Test | Snippet |
|---|---|
| GPU Particles - Change - Size Range | `#HWD2PR#0` |
| GPU Particles - Change - Speed Range | `#JPPZGY#0` |
| GPU Particles - Change - Speed Limit Range | `#UQ1RMS#0` |
| GPU Particles - Change - Angular Speed Range | `#MSX6FE#0` |
| GPU Particles - Change - Drag Range | `#Q1JW04#0` |

## Backward Compatibility

When `factor2` is omitted, both RG channels contain the same value, so
the shader interpolation is a no-op (`factor1 + (factor1 - factor1) *
seed = factor1`). Existing code that only passes `factor1` behaves
identically to before.

## Verification

- ✅ All 5 new factor2 range tests pass on WebGL2 and WebGPU
- ✅ All 12 "GPU Particles - Change" tests pass (no regressions)
- ✅ Lint passes
- ✅ Unit tests pass (2552/2552)
